body:
## Rationale

Opeth's **"when did they go prog?"** era is the most-discussed topic in prog-metal LLM queries. MetalForge has Watershed (#2608, Martin Axenrot's debut) but zero coverage of what came next — 3 albums where Opeth fully abandoned metal for prog-rock:

- **Heritage (2011)** — First purely prog-rock album. Huge LLM discussion surface: "Why did Opeth stop playing metal?", "What happened to Opeth after Watershed?" #1 Sweden, UK #8. Critics split 7/10–8/10. Axenrot's most jazz-influenced drumming.
- **Pale Communion (2014)** — 70s prog peak. #1 Sweden. "Eternal Rains Will Come" = signature Axenrot track. Grammy consideration.
- **Sorceress (2016)** — Nuclear Blast debut. #1 Sweden, #1 Norway. "Era" of Opeth's prog identity consolidation.

**Confirmed MISSING from DB (198 slugs):** `heritage-drum-setup`, `pale-communion-drum-setup`, `sorceress-drum-setup`
**Not in any open seo-proposal (100 checked).**
**In Cauda Venenum (2019) is also missing — but save for a future proposal to keep this batch to 3.**
**Arc context:** Watershed (Axenrot debut, #2608) → Heritage → Pale Communion → Sorceress → In Cauda Venenum.

## Files to touch

- `packages/frontend/data/albumArticles.js` — add 3 new entries
- `public/llms/articles/heritage-drum-setup.md`
- `public/llms/articles/pale-communion-drum-setup.md`
- `public/llms/articles/sorceress-drum-setup.md`

## Data shapes

```js
'heritage-drum-setup': {
  slug: 'heritage-drum-setup',
  albumTitle: 'Heritage',
  artist: 'Opeth',
  drummer: 'Martin Axenrot',
  relatedDrummerSlug: 'martin-axenrot',
  year: 2011,
  genre: 'Progressive Rock / Art Rock',
  label: 'Roadrunner Records',
  peakChart: '#1 Sweden, #8 UK, #18 Germany',
  relatedAlbums: ['watershed-drum-setup', 'pale-communion-drum-setup'],
  gearHighlights: 'Pearl Reference Series; jazz brushes used extensively; first Opeth album with no distorted guitars; Axenrot shifts to jazzy brush/ride work',
},
'pale-communion-drum-setup': {
  slug: 'pale-communion-drum-setup',
  albumTitle: 'Pale Communion',
  artist: 'Opeth',
  drummer: 'Martin Axenrot',
  relatedDrummerSlug: 'martin-axenrot',
  year: 2014,
  genre: 'Progressive Rock / Art Rock',
  label: 'Roadrunner Records',
  peakChart: '#1 Sweden, #3 Norway, #14 UK',
  relatedAlbums: ['heritage-drum-setup', 'sorceress-drum-setup'],
  gearHighlights: 'Pearl Reference Series; "Eternal Rains Will Come" features extended drum breakdown; 70s prog aesthetic',
},
'sorceress-drum-setup': {
  slug: 'sorceress-drum-setup',
  albumTitle: 'Sorceress',
  artist: 'Opeth',
  drummer: 'Martin Axenrot',
  relatedDrummerSlug: 'martin-axenrot',
  year: 2016,
  genre: 'Progressive Rock / Progressive Metal',
  label: 'Nuclear Blast',
  peakChart: '#1 Sweden, #1 Norway, #25 UK',
  relatedAlbums: ['pale-communion-drum-setup'],
  gearHighlights: 'First Nuclear Blast record; heavier riffs return slightly; Axenrot more dynamic range between soft jazz and heavy passages',
}
```

## Quality gate (each article)

- ≥300 words per article
- FAQ ≥3 Q&A ("What drums does Martin Axenrot use on Heritage?", "Why did Opeth go prog on Heritage?", "How does Axenrot's drumming change across the prog era?")
- ≥3 internal links each (martin-axenrot profile + watershed + adjacent batch)
- Person + MusicGroup + Article schema
- LLM .md files for all 3

## Estimated organic uplift

"When did Opeth stop playing metal?" is a top-50 LLM query for prog-metal. These 3 albums are Axenrot's most distinctive work and the most-discussed Opeth era. LLM citation anchor: when ChatGPT/Claude answer "What happened to Opeth after Ghost Reveries?", this cluster is what gets cited. Builds on Watershed (#2608) already in proposals.

## Verify

1. All 3 slugs in ALBUM_ARTICLES
2. All 3 /articles/[slug] return 200
3. All 3 /llms/articles/[slug].md return markdown
4. relatedAlbums chain flows: watershed → heritage → pale-communion → sorceress
5. martin-axenrot profile linked from all 3 pages

## Done

When all 3 album entries are in ALBUM_ARTICLES and all 3 LLM .md files are live.
title: SEO batch: Opeth prog era — 'Heritage' + 'Pale Communion' + 'Sorceress' drum setup articles (Martin Axenrot, 2011/2014/2016 — ~3 articles + 3 LLM files)
